title: Chocolate icebox cookies
subtitle: Slice-and-bake dark chocolate cookies from a chilled dough log
excerpt: Icebox cookies are made by rolling a stiff dough into a log, chilling it until firm, then slicing and baking as needed. The chocolate version uses cocoa powder for a deep, slightly bitter flavour. The dough keeps in the fridge for three days or the freezer for a month, so baking a batch takes ten minutes once the log is made.
sourceNotes: Icebox cookies are an American tradition from the era of ice-box refrigerators in the early twentieth century, when a refrigerated dough log could be stored and sliced to order. They spread into British baking from the 1950s onwards. The chocolate variant using cocoa powder is the most common. This recipe is synthesised from the established American icebox cookie tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the uncut log wrapped in cling film. Slice from frozen, adding 2 to 3 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.batchNotes: Make several logs at once and freeze them for baking throughout the month.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough log can be refrigerated for up to three days before slicing.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan or conventional; check at 10 minutes as thin slices bake quickly

glossaryTerms.definition: After mixing, the dough is turned onto cling film and rolled into a log about 5 cm in diameter. Roll the cling film tightly to compact the log and eliminate air pockets, then twist the ends closed. After chilling, the log slices cleanly with a sharp knife into rounds about 5 to 6 mm thick. If the dough crumbles at the edges when sliced, it is too cold: leave it at room temperature for 5 minutes before continuing.

content.text: The dough is stiff and crumbly when first mixed, which is correct. It comes together when you work it briefly and will slice cleanly once chilled. Form the
tightly so the finished cookies hold a neat round shape.

Beat the 180 g softened butter and 120 g icing sugar together until smooth. Add the egg and 1 tsp vanilla and beat to combine. Sift in the 220 g flour, 40 g cocoa and half a teaspoon salt and mix to a stiff dough. Turn onto a sheet of cling film. Shape into a log about 5 cm in diameter, rolling the cling film around it and twisting the ends closed. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C. Line two baking trays with paper. Slice the chilled log into rounds about 5 mm thick and place 2 cm apart on the trays. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until the edges feel set when lightly pressed; the centres will still be slightly soft. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes before transferring to a rack.

items.solution: The dough was too warm when placed in the oven. Chill the sliced rounds on the tray for 10 minutes before baking, or return the log to the fridge for 30 minutes.

The log is too cold. Leave it at room temperature for 5 to 10 minutes. If the dough crumbles despite being at the right temperature, the original dough was too dry; next time reduce the flour by 10 g.
